name: Fil-C
summary: Filip Pizlo's fanatically-compatible memory-safe C/C++ compiler using invisible capabilities (InvisiCaps) — every 64-bit pointer has shadow capability metadata stored outside the visible address space, looked up on every dereference. Uses FUGC (Fil's Unbelievable Garbage Collector) for concurrent real-time accurate reclamation; ~1.5×–4× slowdown.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Filip Pizlo's Fil-C is a fanatically-compatible memory-safe C/C++ compiler using invisible capabilities (InvisiCaps) — every 64-bit pointer has shadow capability metadata stored outside the visible address space, looked up on every dereference. Thread-safe variant of SoftBound, immune to pointer races (worst case = panic). Stack allocations are heap-allocated to make signal handlers / `longjmp` memory-safe.

Where Profiles and Safe C++ both modify the source language (restriction or borrow checker), Fil-C leaves the source untouched and pays the safety price entirely at runtime through pointer-tagged capabilities and a real-time GC.

sections.title: InvisiCaps and FUGC
sections.content:
InvisiCaps store a shadow capability per pointer — bounds and authority — at a sidecar location outside the address the C source ever sees. Every dereference loads the shadow, checks bounds, and traps on violation. The thread-safe design is what distinguishes it from earlier capability-on-pointer schemes like SoftBound: there is no torn-tag race, the worst-case violation is a deterministic panic.

FUGC (Fil's Unbelievable Garbage Collector) is concurrent, real-time, and accurate; threads are never suspended; freeing zeroes capability bounds so any subsequent dereference of a stale pointer traps. Stack allocations being heap-allocated is what makes signal handlers and `longjmp` memory-safe — a pointer captured during signal delivery cannot dangle when the original stack frame would have unwound.

sections.title: Compatibility and status
sections.content:
Compiles unmodified Linux userspace (Linux From Scratch); supports pthreads, signals, mmap, C++ exceptions, atomics, SIMD intrinsics. Based on Clang 20.1.8. Active single-developer project; ~1.5× slowdown best case, ~4× worst.

Not standardized but increasingly cited as the "what a sound C/C++ memory-safety solution actually looks like" reference point — when commenters argue Profiles cannot be sound without aliasing information, Fil-C is the counterpoint that *runtime* capabilities + GC do achieve soundness on unchanged C source, with the trade-off being slowdown rather than language disruption.
